metadata.title: 2026-2030 Technician Class FCC Element 2 Question Pool

groups.name: Purpose and permissible use of the Amateur Radio Service; Operator/primary station license grant; Meanings of basic terms used in FCC rules; Interference; RACES rules; Phonetics; Frequency Coordinator; Beacon

questions.question: Which of the following is part of the Basis and Purpose of the Amateur Radio Service?

answers.A: Providing personal radio communications for as many citizens as possible
answers.C: Advancing skills in the technical and communication phases of the radio art

questions.question: Which agency regulates and enforces the rules for the Amateur Radio Service in the United States?
What do the FCC rules state regarding the use of a phonetic alphabet for station identification in the Amateur Radio Service?

answers.A: It is required when transmitting emergency messages
answers.B: It is encouraged when using phone emissions
answers.C: It is required when in contact with foreign stations

questions.question: How do you receive official notification of your new license and call sign after passing the exam?

answers.A: Email from the FCC with a link to download the license grant
answers.B: USPS First-Class Mail from the FCC
answers.C: Email from the lead volunteer examiner
answers.D: USPS First-Class Mail from the lead volunteer examiner

questions.question: What proves that the FCC has issued an operator/primary license grant?

answers.A: A printed copy of the certificate of successful completion of examination
answers.B: An email notification from the NCVEC granting the license
answers.C: The license appears in the FCC ULS database

questions.question: On which of the following HF frequencies can automatically controlled amateur propagation beacons be found?

answers.A: Anywhere in the Technician portion of 10 meters
answers.B: Anywhere, if they are registered with the FCC
answers.C: In a specific portion of each of the HF Technician bands
answers.D: On ten meters, between 28.200 MHz and 28.300 MHz

questions.question: What is the FCC Part 97 definition of a space station?

answers.C: An amateur station located more than 50 km above Earth's surface
answers.D: An amateur station using amateur radio satellites for relay of signals

questions.question: Who recommends transmit/receive channels for repeater and auxiliary stations?

answers.A: A Frequency Spectrum Manager appointed by the FCC
answers.B: A Volunteer Frequency Coordinator recognized by local amateurs

questions.question: Who selects a Frequency Coordinator?

answers.A: The FCC Office of Spectrum Management and Coordination Policy
answers.B: The local chapter of the Office of National Council of Independent Frequency Coordinators
answers.C: Amateur operators in a local or regional area whose stations are eligible to be repeater or auxiliary stations

questions.question: Besides an FCC-issued amateur operator license, what is required to be the control operator of a Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Service (RACES) Station?

answers.A: A written recommendation by the local ARRL Emergency Coordinator
answers.B: Membership in the Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES)
answers.C: Certification of current enrollment by a civil defense organization

questions.question: Which of the following is prohibited?

answers.A: International communications on VHF bands and higher frequencies

groups.name: Frequency allocations; Emission modes; Spectrum sharing; Transmissions near band edges; Contacting the International Space Station; Power output

questions.question: Which of the following frequency ranges are available for phone operation by Technician licensees?
Which of the following U.S. amateur radio operators are allowed to contact the International Space Station (ISS) on VHF bands?

answers.A: Only amateurs with a General class or higher license
answers.B: Any amateur with a Technician class or higher license
answers.C: Only amateurs with a General class or higher license, and NASA approval
answers.D: Any amateurs with a Technician class or higher license, and NASA approval

questions.question: Which frequency is in the 6-meter amateur band?
Which amateur band includes 146.52 MHz?
Which of the following bands include frequencies where Technicians are authorized to use digital modes such as FT8?
On which HF bands does a Technician class operator have phone privileges?
Which of the following VHF/UHF band segments are limited to CW only?

answers.A: 50.0 MHz to 50.1 MHz and 144.0 MHz to 144.1 MHz
answers.B: 219 MHz to 220 MHz and 420.0 MHz to 420.1 MHz

questions.question: How are US amateurs restricted in segments of bands where the Amateur Radio Service is secondary?

answers.A: U.S. amateurs may find non-amateur stations in those segments, and must avoid interfering with them
answers.B: U.S. amateurs must give foreign amateur stations priority in those segments
answers.C: International communications are not permitted in those segments
answers.D: Digital transmissions are not permitted in those segments

questions.question: Why should you not set your transmit frequency to be exactly at the edge of an amateur band or sub-band?

answers.A: To allow for calibration error in the transmitter frequency display
answers.B: So that modulation sidebands do not extend beyond the band edge
answers.C: To allow for transmitter frequency drift

questions.question: Where may SSB phone be used in amateur bands above 50 MHz?

answers.A: Only in sub-bands allocated to General class or higher licensees
answers.C: In at least some segment of all these bands
answers.D: On any band if the power is limited to 25 watts

questions.question: What is the maximum peak envelope power output for Technician class operators in their HF band segments?
Except for some specific restrictions, what is the maximum peak envelope power output for Technician class operators using frequencies above 30 MHz?

groups.name: Licensing: classes, sequential and vanity call sign systems, places where the Amateur Radio Service is regulated by the FCC, name and address on FCC license database, term, renewal, grace period, maintaining mailing address; International communications

questions.question: For which classes of amateur radio licenses does the FCC currently issue new licenses?

answers.B: Technician, Technician Plus, General, Amateur Extra

questions.question: Who may select a desired call sign under the vanity call sign rules?

answers.A: Only a licensed amateur with a General or Amateur Extra Class license
answers.B: Only a licensed amateur with an Amateur Extra Class license
answers.C: Only a licensed amateur who has been licensed continuously for more than 10 years

questions.question: What types of international communications are FCC-licensed amateur radio stations permitted to make?

answers.A: Communications incidental to the purposes of the Amateur Radio Service and remarks of a personal character
answers.B: Communications incidental to conducting business or remarks of a personal nature
answers.C: Only communications incidental to contest exchanges; all other communications are prohibited
answers.D: Any communications that would be permitted by an international broadcast station

questions.question: What may happen if the FCC is unable to reach you by email?

answers.A: Fine and suspension of operator license
answers.B: Revocation of the station license or suspension of the operator license
answers.C: Revocation of access to the license record in the FCC system
answers.D: Nothing; there is no such requirement

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid Group D call sign format for Technician class?
Which of the following statements is true about using your amateur radio license when operating aboard a vessel or craft in international waters?

answers.A: Amateur operation is prohibited in international waters
answers.B: You may only operate during maritime emergencies
answers.C: You need special FCC authorization for maritime mobile operation
answers.D: You may operate from a US-documented vessel with the master's permission

questions.question: How long before the expiration date may an amateur radio license renewal be requested?
What is the normal term for an FCC-issued amateur radio license?
What is the grace period for renewal if an amateur license expires?
How soon after passing the examination for your first amateur radio license may you transmit on the amateur radio bands?

answers.A: Immediately on receiving your Certificate of Successful Completion of Examination (CSCE)
answers.B: As soon as your operator/station license grant appears on the ARRL website
answers.C: As soon as your operator/station license grant appears in the FCC’s license database
answers.D: As soon as you receive your license in the mail from the FCC

questions.question: If your license has expired and is still within the allowable grace period, may you continue to transmit on the amateur radio bands?

answers.A: Yes, for up to two years
answers.B: Yes, as soon as you apply for renewal
answers.C: Yes, for up to one year
answers.D: No, you must wait until the license has been renewed

groups.name: Authorized and prohibited transmissions; Sale of equipment

questions.question: With which countries are FCC-licensed amateur radio stations prohibited from exchanging communications?

answers.A: Any country whose administration has notified the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) that it objects to such communications
answers.B: Any country whose administration has notified the American Radio Relay League (ARRL) that it objects to such communications
answers.C: Any country banned from such communications by the International Amateur Radio Union (IARU)
answers.D: Any country banned from making such communications by the American Radio Relay League (ARRL)

questions.question: Under which of the following circumstances are one-way transmissions by an amateur station prohibited?

answers.A: Announcements of upcoming ham radio operating events

questions.question: When is it permissible to transmit messages encoded to obscure their meaning?

answers.A: Only when using a remote station during a contest
answers.B: Only when transmitting certain approved digital codes
answers.C: Only when transmitting control commands to space stations or model craft

questions.question: Under what conditions is an amateur station authorized to transmit music using a phone emission?

answers.A: When incidental to an authorized retransmission of manned spacecraft communications
answers.B: When the music produces no spurious emissions
answers.C: When transmissions are limited to less than three minutes per hour
answers.D: When the music is transmitted above 1280 MHz

questions.question: When may amateur radio operators use their stations to notify other amateurs of the availability of equipment for sale or trade?

answers.B: When the equipment is not the personal property of either the station licensee, or the control operator, or their close relatives
answers.C: When no profit is made on the sale
answers.D: When selling amateur radio equipment and not on a regular basis

questions.question: What, if any, are the restrictions concerning transmission of language that may be considered indecent or obscene?

answers.A: The FCC maintains a list of words that are not permitted to be used on amateur frequencies
answers.C: The International Telecommunications Union (ITU) maintains a list of words that are not permitted to be used on amateur frequencies

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an auxiliary station?

answers.A: A station sending one-way transmissions between a remote repeater receiver and the main repeater transmitter
answers.B: A backup radio for emergency use in case the main station radio fails
answers.C: A station used in the Military Auxiliary Radio System for linking government and amateur stations in a digital network
answers.D: A second station utilized in multi-operator contest stations

questions.question: In which of the following circumstances may the control operator of an amateur station receive compensation for operating that station?

answers.A: When the communication is related to the sale of amateur equipment by the control operator's employer
answers.B: When the communication is a part of classroom instruction at an educational institution
answers.C: When the communication is made to obtain emergency information for a local broadcast station

questions.question: When may amateur stations transmit information in support of broadcasting, program production, or news gathering, assuming no other means is available?

answers.A: When such communications are directly related to the immediate safety of human life or protection of property
answers.B: When broadcasting communications to or from the space shuttle
answers.C: Where non-commercial programming is gathered and supplied exclusively to the National Public Radio network

questions.question: How does the FCC define broadcasting for the Amateur Radio Service?

answers.B: Any transmission made by the licensed station
answers.C: Transmission of messages directed only to amateur operators
answers.D: Transmissions intended for reception by the general public

questions.question: When may an amateur station transmit without identifying on the air?

answers.A: When the transmissions are of a brief nature to make station adjustments
answers.C: When the transmitted power level is below 0.1 watt
answers.D: When transmitting signals to control model craft

groups.name: Control operator: eligibility, designating, privileges, duties, location, required; Control point; Control types: automatic, remote

questions.question: When may an amateur station transmit without a control operator?

answers.A: When using automatic control, such as in the case of a repeater
answers.B: When the station licensee is away and another licensed amateur is using the station
answers.C: When the transmitting station is an auxiliary station

questions.question: Who may be the control operator of a station communicating through an amateur satellite or space station?

answers.A: Only an Amateur Extra Class operator
answers.B: A General class or higher licensee with a satellite operator certification
answers.C: Only an Amateur Extra Class operator who is also an AMSAT member
answers.D: Any amateur allowed to transmit on the satellite uplink frequency

questions.question: Who must designate the station control operator?
What determines the transmitting frequency privileges of an amateur station?

answers.A: The frequency authorized by the frequency coordinator
answers.B: The frequencies printed on the license grant
answers.C: The highest class of operator license held by anyone on the premises
answers.D: The class of operator license held by the control operator

questions.question: What is an amateur station’s control point?

answers.A: The location of the station’s transmitting antenna
answers.B: The location of the station’s transmitting apparatus
answers.C: The location at which the control operator function is performed
answers.D: The mailing address of the station licensee

questions.question: When, other than during an emergency, may a Technician class licensee be the control operator of a station operating in an Amateur Extra Class band segment?

answers.B: When designated as the control operator by an Amateur Extra Class licensee
answers.C: As part of a multi-operator contest team
answers.D: When using a club station whose trustee holds an Amateur Extra Class license

questions.question: When the control operator is not the station licensee, who is responsible for the proper operation of the station?

answers.A: All licensed amateurs who are present at the operation
answers.D: The control operator and the station licensee

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of automatic control?

answers.B: Controlling a station over the internet
answers.C: Using a computer or other device to send CW automatically
answers.D: Using a computer or other device to identify automatically

questions.question: Which amateur stations may be remotely controlled?
Which of the following is an example of remote control as defined in Part 97?

answers.B: Operating the station over the internet
answers.C: Controlling a model aircraft, boat, or car by amateur radio

questions.question: What is a control operator as defined in Part 97?

answers.A: The person speaking or otherwise communicating messages over an amateur station
answers.B: The person who is the licensee of an amateur station
answers.C: An amateur operator identified in the FCC database as responsible for transmissions and FCC rules compliance at a station license location
answers.D: An amateur operator designated by the licensee of a station to be responsible for transmissions and FCC rules compliance at that station

groups.name: Station identification; Repeaters; Third-party communications; Club stations; FCC inspection

questions.question: When must the station licensee make the station and the station records available for inspection?

answers.A: At any time ten days after notification by the FCC of such an inspection
answers.B: At any time upon request by an FCC representative
answers.C: At any time after a written notification by the FCC of such inspection
answers.D: Only when presented with a valid warrant by an FCC official or government agent

questions.question: How often must you identify with your FCC-assigned call sign when using tactical call signs such as “Race Headquarters”?

answers.A: Never, the tactical call is sufficient
answers.C: At least every 10 minutes during and at the end of a communication
answers.D: At the end of every transmission

questions.question: When are you required to transmit your assigned call sign?

answers.A: At the beginning of each contact, and every 10 minutes thereafter
answers.B: At least once during each transmission
answers.C: At least every 15 minutes during and at the end of a communication
answers.D: At least every 10 minutes during and at the end of a communication

questions.question: What language must you use for identification when using a phone emission?

answers.A: Any language recognized by the United Nations
answers.B: Any language recognized by the ITU

questions.question: What method of call sign identification is required for a station transmitting phone signals?

answers.A: Send the call sign followed by the indicator RPT
answers.B: Send the call sign using a CW or phone emission
answers.C: Send the call sign followed by the indicator R
answers.D: Send the call sign using only a phone emission

questions.question: Which of the following self-assigned indicators are acceptable when using a phone transmission?
Which of the following restrictions apply when a non-licensed person speaks to a foreign amateur radio station via a station under the control of an FCC-licensed amateur radio operator?

answers.A: The person must be a U.S. citizen
answers.B: The foreign station must be in a country with which the U.S. has a third-party agreement
answers.C: The licensed control operator must do the station identification

questions.question: What is the definition of third-party communications?

answers.A: A message from a control operator to another amateur station control operator on behalf of another person
answers.B: Amateur radio communications where three stations are in communications with one another
answers.C: Operation when the transmitting equipment is licensed to a person other than the control operator
answers.D: Temporary authorization for an unlicensed person to transmit on the amateur bands for technical experiments

questions.question: What type of amateur station simultaneously retransmits the signal of another amateur station on a different channel or channels?
Who is accountable if a repeater inadvertently retransmits communications that violate the FCC rules?

answers.A: The control operator of the originating station
answers.B: The control operator of the repeater
answers.D: Both the originating station and the repeater owner

questions.question: Which of the following is a requirement for the issuance of a club station license grant?

answers.A: The trustee must have an Amateur Extra Class operator license grant
answers.B: The club must have at least four members
answers.C: The club must be registered with the American Radio Relay League (ARRL)

groups.name: Station operation: choosing an operating frequency, calling another station, test transmissions; Band plans: calling frequencies, repeater offsets

questions.question: What is a common repeater frequency offset in the 2-meter band?
What is the national calling frequency for FM simplex operations in the 2-meter band?
What is a common repeater frequency offset in the 70-centimeter band?
What is an appropriate way to call another station on a repeater if you know the other station's call sign?

answers.A: Say "break, break," then the other station’s call sign, followed by your call sign
answers.B: Say the station's call sign, then identify with your call sign
answers.C: Say "CQ" three times, then the other station's call sign, followed by your call sign
answers.D: Wait for the station to call CQ, then answer with your call sign

questions.question: How should you respond to a station calling CQ?

answers.A: Transmit "CQ" followed by the other station’s call sign
answers.B: Transmit your call sign followed by the other station’s call sign
answers.C: Transmit the other station’s call sign followed by your call sign
answers.D: Transmit a signal report followed by your call sign

questions.question: What is an effective way to seek a call from any phone station when not using a repeater?

answers.A: Sign your call sign once, followed by the words “listening for a call;” if there is no answer, change the frequency and repeat
answers.B: Say “QTC,” followed by “this is” and your call sign; if there is no answer, change the frequency and repeat
answers.C: Transmit an unmodulated carrier for approximately 10 seconds, followed by “this is” and your call sign, and pause to listen; repeat as necessary
answers.D: Repeat “CQ” a few times, followed by “this is,” and your call sign, then pause to listen; repeat as necessary

questions.question: What does the term "repeater offset" mean?

answers.A: The difference between a repeater’s transmit and receive frequencies
answers.B: The repeater has a time delay to prevent interference
answers.C: The approximately half-second delay to allow for the “squelch tail” to be removed
answers.D: The fee charged by the repeater owner or club to provide for maintenance

questions.question: What is the meaning of the procedural signal “CQ”?

answers.A: A shortened form of the term “Contest QSO”
answers.B: “Communication Quality,” used to indicate transmitted audio clarity
answers.C: Only the called station should transmit

questions.question: Which of the following is a customary way to indicate a station is listening on a repeater and looking for a contact?

answers.A: “CQ CQ” followed by the repeater’s call sign
answers.B: The station’s call sign followed by the word “listening”
answers.C: The repeater’s call sign followed by the station’s call sign

questions.question: What is a band plan, beyond the privileges established by the FCC?

answers.A: A voluntary guideline for using different modes or activities within an amateur band
answers.C: A list of available net frequencies
answers.D: A plan devised by a club to indicate frequency band usage

questions.question: What term describes an amateur station that is transmitting and receiving on the same frequency?

groups.name: VHF/UHF operating practices: FM repeater, simplex, reverse splits; Access tones: CTCSS, DTMF; DMR operation; Resolving operating problems; Q signals

questions.question: What is the purpose of the reverse function on a VHF/UHF transceiver?

answers.C: To listen on a repeater’s input frequency
answers.D: To listen on a repeater’s output frequency

questions.question: What term describes the use of a sub-audible tone transmitted along with normal voice audio to open the squelch of a receiver?
Which of the following describes a linked repeater network?

answers.A: A network of repeaters in which signals received by one repeater are transmitted by all the repeaters in the network
answers.B: A single repeater with more than one receiver
answers.C: Multiple repeaters with the same control operator
answers.D: A system of repeaters linked by APRS

questions.question: Which of the following could be the reason you are unable to access a repeater whose output you can hear?

answers.B: You are using the wrong CTCSS tone
answers.C: You are using the wrong DCS code

questions.question: Which of the following would cause your FM transmission audio to drop out on voice peaks?

answers.B: Your FM deviation is too low
answers.D: Your transmit power is too high

questions.question: What type of signaling to a repeater uses two simultaneous audio tones?
How can you join a digital repeater’s “talkgroup”?

answers.A: Register with the local Frequency Coordinator
answers.B: Register with the digital repeater control operator
answers.C: Program your radio with the group’s ID or code
answers.D: Program your radio with the DTMF tone used by the talkgroup

questions.question: Which of the following applies when two stations transmitting on the same frequency interfere with each other?

answers.A: The stations should negotiate continued use of the frequency
answers.B: Both stations should choose another frequency to avoid conflict
answers.C: Whichever station was on the frequency first has preemptive rights to the frequency
answers.D: Use subaudible tones so both stations can share the frequency

questions.question: Why are simplex channels designated in the VHF/UHF band plans?

answers.A: So stations within range of each other can communicate without tying up a repeater
answers.D: So stations with simple transmitters can access the repeater without automated offset

questions.question: Which Q signal indicates that you are receiving interference from other stations?
Which Q signal indicates that you are changing frequency?
What is the digital color code used on DMR repeater systems?

answers.A: An access code which must be programmed into a DMR transmitter to access a specific repeater
answers.B: A code which automatically programs the repeater offset to a frequency chosen by the user
answers.C: A code which identifies the specific CODEC being used so that the repeater can properly decode the audio
answers.D: A code transmitted by the repeater to indicate health and status of the equipment

questions.question: What is the purpose of a squelch function?

answers.A: Reduce a CW transmitter's key clicks
answers.B: Mute the receiver audio when a signal is not present
answers.C: Eliminate parasitic oscillations in an RF amplifier

questions.question: Which of the following is a “talkgroup”?

answers.A: A group of amateur radio operators who share email on a specific amateur radio topic
answers.B: An identifier used by DMR to organize radio traffic so that those who want to hear the group aren’t bothered by other radio traffic
answers.C: The members of a net organized for amateur radio operators with a specific common interest
answers.D: A method of automatically alerting all group members to the frequency where any member is transmitting

groups.name: Public service: emergency operations, applicability of FCC rules, RACES and ARES, net and traffic procedures, operating restrictions during emergencies, use of phonetics in message handling

questions.question: When do the FCC Part 97 Amateur Radio Service rules NOT apply to the operation of an amateur station?
Which of the following are typical duties of a Net Control Station?

answers.A: Choose the regular net meeting time and frequency
answers.B: Ensure that all stations checking into the net are properly licensed for operation on the net frequency
answers.C: Call the net to order and direct communications between stations checking in

questions.question: What technique is used to ensure that voice messages containing unusual words are received correctly?

answers.A: Send the words by voice and Morse code
answers.B: Use the Q-code “QSR” to ask the receiving station to repeat the words back
answers.C: Spell the words using a standard phonetic alphabet

questions.question: What is RACES?

answers.A: An emergency organization combining amateur radio and citizens band operators and frequencies
answers.C: A radio contest held in a short period, sometimes called a “sprint”
answers.D: An FCC Part 97 amateur radio service for civil defense communications during national emergencies

questions.question: What does the term “traffic” refer to in net operation?

answers.A: Formal messages exchanged by net stations
answers.B: The number of stations checking in and out of a net
answers.C: Operation by mobile or portable stations
answers.D: A count of the number of activations of the net each month

questions.question: What is the Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES)?

answers.A: A group of licensed amateurs who have voluntarily registered their qualifications and equipment for communications duty in the public service
answers.B: A group of licensed amateurs who are members of the military and who voluntarily agreed to provide message handling services in the case of an emergency
answers.C: A training program that provides licensing courses for those interested in obtaining an amateur license to use during emergencies
answers.D: A training program that certifies amateur operators for membership in the Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Service

questions.question: Which of the following is standard practice when you participate in a net?

answers.A: When first responding to the net control station, transmit your call sign, name, and address as in the FCC database
answers.B: Record the time of each of your transmissions
answers.C: Unless you are reporting an emergency, transmit only when directed by the net control station

questions.question: Which of the following relays messages using email addresses based on amateur callsigns?
Are amateur station control operators ever permitted to operate outside the frequency privileges of their license class?

answers.B: Yes, but only when part of a FEMA emergency plan
answers.C: Yes, but only when part of a RACES emergency plan
answers.D: Yes, but only in situations involving the immediate safety of human life or protection of property

questions.question: What information is contained in the preamble of a formal traffic message?

answers.A: The email address of the originating station
answers.B: The address of the intended recipient
answers.C: The telephone number of the addressee
answers.D: Information needed to track the message

questions.question: What is meant by “check” in a radiogram header?

answers.A: The number of words or word equivalents in the text portion of the message
answers.B: The call sign of the originating station
answers.C: A list of stations that have relayed the message
answers.D: A box on the message form that indicates that the message was received and/or relayed

groups.name: Radio wave characteristics: how a radio signal travels, fading, multipath, polarization, wavelength vs absorption; Antenna orientation

questions.question: Why do VHF signal strengths sometimes vary greatly when the antenna is moved only a few feet?

answers.A: The signal path encounters different concentrations of water vapor
answers.B: VHF ionospheric propagation is very sensitive to path length
answers.C: Multipath propagation cancels or reinforces signals
answers.D: The Doppler effect causes slight frequency shifts which result in changes in signal strength

questions.question: How does vegetation affect UHF and microwave signals?

answers.A: Causes knife-edge diffraction, distorting voice peaks
answers.B: Absorbs signals, leading to poor reception of weak signals
answers.C: Amplifies signals, improving reception of weak signals

questions.question: What antenna polarization is normally used for long-distance CW and SSB contacts on the VHF and UHF bands?
What is the effect of antenna cross-polarization over a line-of-sight VHF or UHF path?
When using a directional antenna, how might your station be able to communicate with a distant repeater if buildings or obstructions are blocking the direct line of sight path?

answers.A: Change from vertical to horizontal polarization
answers.B: Try to find a path that reflects signals to the repeater

questions.question: What is the meaning of the term “picket fencing”?

answers.A: Alternating transmissions during a net operation
answers.B: Rapid flutter on mobile signals due to multipath propagation
answers.C: A type of ground system used with vertical antennas
answers.D: Interference from cable TV in the form of carriers at fixed intervals across the band

questions.question: What weather condition might decrease range at microwave frequencies?
What is a likely cause of irregular fading of signals propagated by the ionosphere?

answers.A: Frequency shift due to Faraday rotation
answers.D: Random combining of signals arriving via different paths

questions.question: Which of the following results from the fact that signals propagated by the ionosphere are elliptically polarized?

answers.B: Either vertically or horizontally polarized antennas may be used for transmission or reception
answers.D: Both the transmitting and receiving antennas must be of the same polarization

questions.question: What effect does multi-path propagation have on data transmissions?

answers.A: Transmission rates must be increased by a factor equal to the number of separate paths observed
answers.B: Transmission rates must be decreased by a factor equal to the number of separate paths observed
answers.C: No significant changes will occur if the signals are transmitted using FM
answers.D: Error rates are likely to increase

questions.question: Which region of the atmosphere can reflect HF radio waves?
What effect does fog or rain have on 10-meter and 6-meter band signals?

questions.question: What is the relationship between the electric and magnetic fields of an electromagnetic wave?
What property of a radio wave defines its polarization?

answers.A: The orientation of the electric field
answers.B: The orientation of the magnetic field
answers.C: The ratio of the energy in the magnetic field to the energy in the electric field
answers.D: The ratio of the velocity to the wavelength
